# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based HR management system built with TypeScript and Vite. It follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns between UI components, business logic, and data management.

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Routing**: React Router v6 with protected routes and role-based access control
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state, React Context for global state
- **UI Library**: shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives with Tailwind CSS
- **Form Handling**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and CSS variables

### Backend Integration
- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL with real-time capabilities)
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with email/password and magic link support
- **API**: Supabase client for database operations and real-time subscriptions
- **File Storage**: Supabase Storage for avatars and document uploads

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Component Organization
- **Atomic Design**: Components organized by complexity (ui/, layout/, feature-specific/)
- **Composition over Inheritance**: Extensive use of React composition patterns
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic extracted into reusable hooks (useAuth, use-toast)
- **Utility Functions**: Pure functions in lib/ directory (utils.ts, role-access.ts)

### Data Flow
- **Server State**: TanStack Query manages API calls with caching and optimistic updates
- **Real-time Updates**: Supabase subscriptions for live data synchronization
- **Form State**: React Hook Form manages local form state with validation
- **Global State**: Context providers for auth state and theme management

### Security & Access Control
- **Role-Based Access**: canAccess() function checks permissions based on user role
- **Protected Routes**: Route-level protection with automatic redirects
- **Database RLS**: Row Level Security policies in Supabase
- **Input Validation**: Zod schemas validate all user inputs

## Database Schema Highlights

### Core Entities
- **companies**: Multi-tenant company management
- **employees**: Employee profiles with role assignments
- **departments**: Organizational structure
- **projects**: Project management with assignments
- **clients**: Client relationship management
- **invoices**: Invoice generation and tracking
- **evaluations**: Performance evaluation system (quarterly and daily)
- **payroll**: Automated payroll processing
- **hr_policies**: Document management with rich text content

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies and departments
- Projects have many-to-many relationship with employees
- Evaluations link employees as reviewers and reviewees
- Invoices connect projects, clients, and employees

## Component Patterns

### UI Components (shadcn/ui)
- Consistent design system with customizable variants
- Accessible components built on Radix UI
- Dark/light theme support with CSS variables
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach

### Business Components
- **EvaluationTimeline**: Complex component managing multiple evaluation types
- **SearchableEmployeeSelect**: Reusable employee selection with search
- **RichTextEditor**: Tiptap-based editor for policy documents
- **AppLayout**: Main application shell with sidebar navigation

### Layout Components
- **AppSidebar**: Navigation with role-based menu items
- **TopBar**: User actions and notifications
- **ProtectedRoute**: HOC for route protection

## Development Workflow

### Code Quality
- **TypeScript**: Strict type checking enabled
- **ESLint**: Code linting with React-specific rules
- **Prettier**: Code formatting (implied by ESLint config)
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E tests

### Build & Deployment
- **Vite**: Fast development server with HMR
- **Production Build**: Optimized bundles with code splitting
- **Environment Config**: Separate configs for development/production

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap
- `src/App.tsx`: Main component with routing configuration

### Core Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking logic
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Utility functions (cn() for class merging)
- `src/integrations/supabase/`: Database client and type definitions

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Styling configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration

### Components
- `src/components/ui/`: Reusable UI primitives
- `src/components/layout/`: Application layout components
- `src/components/[feature]/`: Feature-specific components

## Business Logic Insights

### Evaluation System
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Formal performance reviews with recommendations
- **Daily Evaluations**: Quick feedback system with direction (positive/constructive)
- **Visibility Rules**: Role-based access to evaluation data
- **Timeline View**: Unified display of all evaluation types

### Payroll Processing
- Automated calculation based on attendance and salary components
- Support for allowances, overtime, and deductions
- PDF generation for payslips
- Integration with evaluation scores for bonuses

### Invoice Management
- Project-based invoicing with client details
- Automatic PDF generation
- Email notifications with attachments
- Status tracking and payment management

## Performance Considerations

- **Code Splitting**: Vite handles automatic code splitting
- **Query Optimization**: TanStack Query caches API responses
- **Image Optimization**: Lazy loading and responsive images
- **Bundle Analysis**: Build tools provide bundle size insights

## Testing Strategy

- **Unit Tests**: Component and utility function testing
- **Integration Tests**: API integration and component interactions
- **E2E Tests**: Playwright for critical user journeys
- **Test Coverage**: Focus on business logic and user interactions

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the codebase structure, architectural decisions, and development patterns used in Beudox HR.
